title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: U.S. Economy Grew Modestly at End of 2025
items.description: Gross domestic product expanded at a 1.4 percent annual rate in the last quarter of the year, hit by the effects of the government shutdown.

items.title: GDP growth sharply slows to 1.4%, less than half of fourth quarter expectations
items.description: Economists say the small bump in unemployment rates is due to the Trump Administration's immigration crackdown stalling population growth.

items.title: This math graduate applied to over 1,000 jobs and still didn’t hear back—so he moved to Austria
items.description: Gen Z and millennial workers in Britain are so fed up with being ghosted by employers that some are giving up on the country altogether.

items.title: Head of Russia’s Antidoping Agency Was Involved in 2014 Doping Scheme, Whistle-Blower Claims
items.description: The accusation surfaced as Russia tries to re-establish its Olympics eligibility.

items.title: First, Colbert, now ‘The View’: Trump’s FCC opens equal time investigation into political chat show
items.description: “The FCC has an enforcement action underway on that,” Chairman Brendan Carr told reporters after an agency meeting Wednesday.

items.title: How Modi’s AI handholding moment backfired as Sam and Dario refused to play along

items.title: A Cancer Detection Test Fails in Major Study
items.description: A closely watched clinical trial in Britain that screened blood for early detection of cancer did not show a reduction in later stages of the disease.

items.title: As Trump Considers Second Iran Attack, It Could Be Deadlier Than the First
items.description: Within days of the U.S. strikes on Iran’s nuclear facilities last year, all sides agreed to a cease-fire. This time could be different.

items.title: A New U.S. Blockade Is Strangling Cuba
